annotations.label: Cube

anchor.snippet: A cube is a polyhedron with eight vertices

annotations.note: Mathlib only has the topological unit cube I^N = N→I (with its boundary), not the geometric solid with eight vertices, twelve edges, and six faces.

annotations.label: Cube as a special case of a cuboid

anchor.snippet: A cube is a special case of a rectangular cuboid

annotations.note: No geometric cuboid/rectangular box solid is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dihedral angle is a right angle

anchor.snippet: Both polyhedra have the same dihedral angle

annotations.note: Dihedral angles of polyhedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic of the cube is 2

anchor.snippet: the cube has Euler characteristic of 2

annotations.note: The polyhedral Euler formula V−E+F=2 is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cube as an orthogonal polyhedron

anchor.snippet: the cube is classified as an orthogonal polyhedron

annotations.note: Orthogonal polyhedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cube as special case of parallelepiped, rhombohedron, trapezohedron

anchor.snippet: The cube is a special case of other cuboids

annotations.note: A `parallelepiped` (set spanned by vectors) is defined, of which a cube is a special case, but the specialization, rhombohedron, and trapezohedron are not formalized.

annotations.label: Non-composite (elementary) polyhedron

anchor.snippet: The cube is a non-composite or an elementary polyhedron

annotations.note: Non-composite/elementary polyhedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Face diagonal of the cube

anchor.snippet: the face diagonal of the cube is the diagonal of a square

annotations.note: The face diagonal of a cube is not formalized.

annotations.label: Space diagonal of the cube

anchor.snippet: the space diagonal of the cube is a line connecting two vertices that are not in the same face

annotations.note: The space diagonal of a cube is not formalized.

annotations.label: Surface area of the cube

anchor.snippet: is six times the area of a square

annotations.note: Surface area of polyhedra is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Volume of the cube

anchor.snippet: the formula for the volume of a cube is the third power of its side length

annotations.note: The Lebesgue volume of an axis-aligned box is the product of its side lengths, so the cube's s³ is a special case, but it is not stated for the cube.

annotations.label: Closed geodesics on a cube

anchor.snippet: The cube has three types of closed geodesics

annotations.note: Geodesics on the surface of a cube are not formalized.

annotations.label: Insphere, midsphere, and circumsphere

anchor.snippet: is a sphere tangent to the faces of a cube at their centroids

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Affine.Simplex.circumsphere` for simplices only, but no insphere/midsphere/circumsphere for a cube.

annotations.label: Unit cube

anchor.snippet: A unit cube is a cube with 1 unit in length along each edge

annotations.note: The unit cube is present as the topological space I^N = N→I with I=[0,1], generalized over the index type, but not treated as a metric/geometric solid.

annotations.label: Prince Rupert's cube

anchor.snippet: the edges of a cube passing through the unit cube's hole could be as large as approximately 1.06 units in length

annotations.note: Prince Rupert's cube is not formalized.

annotations.label: Doubling the cube

anchor.snippet: An ancient problem of doubling the cube requires the construction of a cube

annotations.note: Field-theoretic straightedge-and-compass constructibility (impossibility of doubling the cube) is not in Mathlib; the existing `Constructible` decls are topological/scheme-theoretic.

annotations.label: Octahedral symmetry of order 48

anchor.snippet: the cube has 48 isometries

annotations.note: The type-B Coxeter matrix is defined (its group for n=3 is the order-48 hyperoctahedral symmetry of the cube), but neither the order nor the link to the cube is established.

annotations.label: Dual polyhedron is the regular octahedron

anchor.snippet: the dual polyhedron of a cube is the regular octahedron

annotations.note: Polyhedral duality and the octahedron are not formalized.

annotations.label: Cube is a regular polyhedron

anchor.snippet: The cube is face-transitive

annotations.note: Regular polyhedra and face/edge/vertex transitivity of solids are not formalized.

annotations.label: Vertex configuration and Schläfli symbol

anchor.snippet: Each vertex is surrounded by three squares

annotations.note: Vertex configurations and Schläfli symbols are not formalized.

annotations.label: Platonic solids

anchor.snippet: The Platonic solids are five polyhedra known since antiquity

annotations.note: The Platonic solids are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euclid's ratio of circumscribed sphere to edge

anchor.snippet: showed how to find the ratio of the circumscribed sphere's diameter to the edge length

annotations.note: This ratio for the cube is not formalized.

annotations.label: Eleven nets of the cube

anchor.snippet: The cube has eleven different nets

annotations.note: Nets/unfoldings of polyhedra are not formalized.

annotations.label: Cartesian coordinates of cube vertices

anchor.snippet: the Cartesian coordinates of the vertices are

annotations.note: The {±1}³ vertex coordinates of a cube are not formalized as such.

annotations.label: Interior of the cube

anchor.snippet: Its interior consists of all points

annotations.note: The interior of a geometric cube is not formalized.

annotations.label: Surface of a cube as a locus

anchor.snippet: A cube's surface with center

annotations.note: The cube's surface as the max-norm locus is not formalized (no identification of a sup-norm sphere as a cube).

annotations.label: Cube as a Hanner polytope

anchor.snippet: The cube is a Hanner polytope

annotations.note: Hanner polytopes are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Steinitz's theorem

anchor.snippet: It is attainable according to Steinitz's theorem

annotations.note: Steinitz's theorem on polytopal graphs is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cubical graph

anchor.snippet: is called the cubical graph

annotations.note: `SimpleGraph` exists generally but the specific Q₃ cubical graph is not defined.

annotations.label: Cubical graph as a prism graph

anchor.snippet: The cubical graph is also classified as a prism graph

annotations.note: Prism graphs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cubical graph as a hypercube graph

anchor.snippet: The cubical graph is a special case of hypercube graph

annotations.note: Hypercube graphs Qₙ are not defined as graphs in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hamiltonian cycle of the cubical graph

anchor.snippet: it has a cycle which visits every vertex exactly once

annotations.note: The Hamiltonian-cycle predicate is formalized in general but not applied to the (undefined) cubical graph.

annotations.label: Cubical graph as a unit distance graph

anchor.snippet: it is also an example of a unit distance graph

annotations.note: Unit distance graphs are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cubical graph is bipartite

anchor.snippet: The cubical graph is bipartite

annotations.note: A general bipartite-graph predicate exists, but not the claim for the cubical graph.

annotations.label: Cubical graph is not complete bipartite

anchor.snippet: this bipartite graph is not complete

annotations.note: `completeBipartiteGraph` is defined in `SimpleGraph.Basic`, but the statement that the cubical graph is not complete bipartite is not.

annotations.label: Crown graph and bipartite Kneser graph

anchor.snippet: It is an example of both a crown graph and a bipartite Kneser graph

annotations.note: Crown graphs and Kneser graphs are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equiprojective polyhedron

anchor.snippet: A polyhedron is considered equiprojective

annotations.note: Equiprojective polyhedra are not formalized.

annotations.label: Cube is equiprojective

anchor.snippet: The cube is equiprojective

annotations.note: Equiprojectivity of the cube is not formalized.

annotations.label: Configuration matrix of the cube

anchor.snippet: The cube can be represented as a configuration matrix

annotations.note: Polytope configuration matrices are not formalized.

annotations.label: Faceting a cube yields the stellated octahedron

anchor.snippet: When faceting a cube

annotations.note: Faceting and the stellated octahedron are not formalized.

annotations.label: Cube as component of two Johnson solids

anchor.snippet: The cube is thus a component of two Johnson solids

annotations.note: Johnson solids are not formalized.

annotations.label: Kleetope of the cube (tetrakis hexahedron)

anchor.snippet: Attaching a low pyramid to each face of a cube produces its Kleetope

annotations.note: Kleetopes and the tetrakis hexahedron are not formalized.

annotations.label: Barycentric subdivision (disdyakis dodecahedron)

anchor.snippet: The barycentric subdivision of a cube

annotations.note: Barycentric subdivision of the cube and the disdyakis dodecahedron are not formalized.

annotations.label: Trirectangular tetrahedron from a cube corner

anchor.snippet: The corner region of a cube can also be truncated by a plane

annotations.note: The trirectangular tetrahedron from a cube corner is not formalized.

annotations.label: Snub cube

anchor.snippet: The snub cube is an Archimedean solid

annotations.note: Archimedean solids such as the snub cube are not formalized.

annotations.label: Truncated cube

anchor.snippet: Each of the cube's vertices can be truncated

annotations.note: The truncated cube is not formalized.

annotations.label: Rhombicuboctahedron

anchor.snippet: When its edges are truncated, it is a rhombicuboctahedron

annotations.note: The rhombicuboctahedron is not formalized.

annotations.label: Expanded cube

anchor.snippet: can also be constructed by separating the cube's faces and then spreading away

annotations.note: The expanded cube is not formalized.

annotations.label: Chamfered cube

anchor.snippet: The chamfered cube is constructed from a cube

annotations.note: The chamfered cube is not formalized.

annotations.label: Regular icosahedron from golden rectangles

anchor.snippet: Three mutually perpendicular golden rectangles can be constructed

annotations.note: The icosahedron construction from golden rectangles is not formalized.

annotations.label: Polycube

anchor.snippet: A polycube is a solid figure formed by joining one or more equal cubes face-to-face

annotations.note: Polycubes are not formalized.

annotations.label: Dalí cross

anchor.snippet: the resulting polycube is the Dalí cross

annotations.note: The Dalí cross polycube is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cube as a hypercube (3-cube)

anchor.snippet: A cube is a three-dimensional instance of a hypercube

annotations.note: The n-cube I^N = N→I generalizes the 3-cube as a topological space, but the polytopal hypercube structure is not formalized.

annotations.label: Space-filling polyhedron

anchor.snippet: A cube is a space-filling polyhedron

annotations.note: Space-filling polyhedra/tilings of this kind are not formalized.

annotations.label: Plesiohedron

anchor.snippet: The cube is a plesiohedron

annotations.note: Plesiohedra are not formalized.

annotations.label: Parallelohedra

anchor.snippet: The plesiohedra include the parallelohedra

annotations.note: Parallelohedra are not formalized.

annotations.label: Every parallelohedron is a zonohedron

anchor.snippet: Every three-dimensional parallelohedron is a zonohedron

annotations.note: Neither parallelohedra nor zonohedra are formalized.

annotations.label: Cubic honeycomb

anchor.snippet: is a cubic honeycomb that consists of four cubes around its edges

annotations.note: The cubic honeycomb tiling is not formalized.

annotations.label: Non-Euclidean cubic honeycombs

anchor.snippet: the honeycomb with three cubes around its edges in a three-dimensional sphere

annotations.note: Non-Euclidean cubic honeycombs are not formalized.

annotations.label: Honeycomb when Dehn invariant is zero

anchor.snippet: Any parallelepiped, including a cube, can achieve a honeycomb if its Dehn invariant is zero

annotations.note: The Dehn invariant and this honeycomb criterion are not formalized.

annotations.label: Hilbert's third problem and the Dehn invariant

anchor.snippet: The Dehn invariant's inception dates back to Hilbert's third problem

annotations.note: Hilbert's third problem and the Dehn invariant are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uniform polyhedron compounds of cubes

anchor.snippet: in which the cubes share the same centre, are uniform polyhedron compounds

annotations.note: Uniform polyhedron compounds are not formalized.

annotations.label: Spherical cube

anchor.snippet: The spherical cube represents the spherical polyhedron

annotations.note: Spherical polyhedra are not formalized.

annotations.label: Vector equilibrium of the spherical cube

anchor.snippet: It has vector equilibrium

annotations.note: Vector equilibrium of a spherical cube is not formalized.

annotations.label: Three-dimensional torus

anchor.snippet: The topological object three-dimensional torus is a topological space

annotations.note: Mathlib has `AddCircle` but no named 3-torus obtained by gluing opposite faces of a cube.

annotations.label: Menger sponge

anchor.snippet: The Menger sponge is an example of a fractal-shaped cube

annotations.note: The Menger sponge fractal is not formalized.

annotations.label: Jerusalem cube and Mosely snowflake

anchor.snippet: Other varieties are the Jerusalem cube and Mosely snowflake

annotations.note: The Jerusalem cube and Mosely snowflake fractals are not formalized.

annotations.label: Subdivision into six square pyramids

anchor.snippet: A cube can be subdivided into six square pyramids

annotations.note: Subdivision of a cube into six square pyramids is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cartesian product of graphs

anchor.snippet: by using the Cartesian product of graphs

annotations.note: Mathlib's `SimpleGraph.boxProd` (□) is the Cartesian product of simple graphs.

annotations.label: Cartesian product of three line segments

anchor.snippet: it can be constructed by using the Cartesian product of three line segments

annotations.note: The cube as a product of three intervals in ℝ³ is not formalized as a named polytope.

annotations.label: Polar reciprocation (dual polyhedron)

anchor.snippet: by a process known as polar reciprocation

annotations.note: Polar reciprocation/duality of polyhedra is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dual polyhedra share the same symmetry group

anchor.snippet: the polyhedron and its dual share their three-dimensional symmetry point group

annotations.note: Symmetry point groups of polyhedra and their duals are not formalized.
